item.title: Poland arrests SIM-swapping gang; Anthropic tests mobile Claude Cowork
item.content:encoded:
Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft — Should Read

Polish authorities arrested four alleged members of a SIM-swapping cybercrime gang accused of breaching telecom partners and hijacking email accounts to steal millions from cryptocurrency exchange accounts. The operation was supported by the FBI and HSI, and the suspects face up to 25 years in prison.

  • Polish authorities arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking employee email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks.
  • The Polish Cybercrime Bureau carried out the operation with support from the FBI and Homeland Security Investigations in the United States.
  • Investigators said the suspects hijacked victims’ phone numbers, intercepted SMS messages and email communications, and gained control of accounts at cryptocurrency exchanges.
  • Authorities estimate that millions of U.S. dollars were stolen and laundered through multiple bank accounts in various countries and digital wallets.
  • The four arrested individuals are in pre-trial detention and face charges related to participation in an organized criminal group, hacking into IT systems to commit theft, and money laundering.

Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile — Optional

Anthropic appears to be testing mobile support for Claude Cowork, letting users start, monitor, and steer long-running desktop-based Claude tasks from their phones. The mobile app likely acts as a remote control while the actual work continues in the background on the user’s computer.

  • Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing users to manage long-running Claude tasks from a phone.
  • Claude Cowork is a desktop-focused agentic mode for Claude that can use files, create documents, generate spreadsheets, write reports, and continue working in the background.
  • Screenshots posted on X indicate that users may be able to start and steer tasks from a phone and check in from a phone, browser, or Claude desktop app.
  • The screenshots state that Claude Cowork work continues in the background even when the mobile app is closed.
  • The article says Claude Cowork on mobile appears to function as a remote control for Cowork on a PC, with the computer still doing the actual work.
